headers.name: Authorization
headers.description: RFC 6750 bearer token header. Carries the JWT; identity rebuilds from this on every hop. Not used on AMQP (broker uses connection-level credentials).

headers.name: Idempotency-Key
headers.description: Idempotency key for request deduplication. Conventional Stripe-style header name.

headers.name: X-Correlation-Id
headers.description: Request correlation id. Inbound value (capped at 128 chars) flows to the response ProblemDetails extensions[correlationId] field; when absent / over-cap a fresh GUID is generated. Echoed back via the same header so callers can include it in subsequent requests / log lines for end-to-end correlation. The X- prefix is retained for compatibility with the widely-deployed convention (Microsoft, AWS, others) despite RFC 6648's deprecation guidance.

headers.name: X-D2-Client-Fingerprint
headers.description: Client-computed device fingerprint for rate limiting + adaptive auth.

headers.name: X-D2-Internal-Token
headers.description: The BFF's boundary token for its calls to Edge — an OAuth client_credentials JWT (audience d2.edge, 15-min TTL) the BFF acquires as an external client of Edge. Sent on outbound BFF->Edge calls so Edge can distinguish BFF-originated SSR traffic from browser-direct traffic in audit + telemetry tags.

headers.name: content-type
headers.description: Always application/octet-stream for D2 messages — body is encrypted bytes (or proto-canonical JSON bytes, never a directly-readable structured type).

headers.name: x-proto-type
headers.description: Fully-qualified proto type name (e.g. d2.events.files.v1.FileUploadedEvent). Lets consumers fail-fast on a type mismatch before deserialization.

headers.name: message-id
headers.description: UUIDv7 message identifier — sortable, includes timestamp, suitable as an idempotency key.

headers.name: traceparent
headers.description: W3C traceparent header — full {version}-{traceId}-{spanId}-{flags} string per https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context. Consumers parse it to start a child span whose parent is the producer's span, so cross-hop trace assembly works in any OTel backend without bespoke linking.

headers.name: tracestate
headers.description: W3C tracestate header (vendor-specific tracing context that rides alongside traceparent). Forwarded as-is so non-D2 tracing systems chained through us don't lose state.

headers.name: x-d2-context
headers.description: Base64url-of-JSON encoded PropagatedContext — small set of cross-hop fields a downstream consumer can't recompute (request id, original request path, fingerprints, WhoIs hash). Same header name + encoding on AMQP / gRPC / HTTP. Identity fields are NEVER in here — they rebuild from the JWT at every hop.

headers.name: x-d2-encryption-kid
headers.description: Encryption key id (kid). Duplicated from the encrypted frame so ops can decide whether archive keys are needed for a stuck DLQ message without decrypting first.

headers.name: x-d2-failure-reason
headers.description: Failure reason metadata attached by the consumer when nacking a message to its DLQ. JSON-encoded DlqFailureMetadata.

headers.name: Accept-Language
headers.description: Standard HTTP locale-preference header (RFC 7231 §5.3.5). Set by browser or OS. Represents the lowest-priority tier of the user locale resolution cascade: profile > org > X-D2-Locale > Accept-Language > fallback 'en-US'. Adding as a typed constant so middleware stops using magic strings.

headers.name: X-D2-Locale
headers.description: D2-specific user-selected locale override (IETF BCP 47 tag, e.g. 'en-US', 'zh-Hans-SG'). Set when the user explicitly picks a locale in the UI. Takes precedence over Accept-Language in the locale cascade. Consumed by Edge auth middleware to populate IRequestContext.LocaleIetfBcp47Tag.

headers.name: X-D2-Timezone
headers.description: Browser-derived IANA timezone name (from Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone, e.g. 'America/New_York'). Set by the BFF fetch interceptor on every request. Used by Edge auth middleware as the third tier of the timezone cascade: profile > org > X-D2-Timezone > WhoIs-derived > fallback 'UTC'.

headers.name: X-D2-Currency
headers.description: User-selected currency override (ISO 4217 code, e.g. 'USD', 'EUR'). No browser-native equivalent — D2-custom. Takes precedence over country-derived currency in the cascade: profile > org > X-D2-Currency > CountryIso31661Alpha2Code-derived > fallback NULL.
